name: Date Sandwich Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Have dates at room temperature. Slice thin and break up in a pan.
2. Combine dates, water, orange peel, and juices in a saucepan. Blend well.
Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until thickened, about 10 minutes. Cool.
3. Measure flour into a large bowl. Add remaining ingredients except margarine. Stir well to blend.
4. Cut in margarine until particles are the size of peas. Press with the back of a fork.
5. Spread half crumb mixture in greased 8 x 8 inches pan. Press down.
6. Spread cooled date filling evenly.
7. Cover with remaining crumbs, pat lightly.
8. Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes until golden.
9. Cool in the pan on a rack. Cut into 18 bars.
(Sugartwin version)
1 bar = 1 1/2 fruit, 1 fat, 97 calories from High Fiber Cookbook - a bit crumbly but delicious.
